candidates.why: This appears directly beneath 'Selling expenses' with a matching balancing line, indicating it is a component already embedded in the selected parent row 'Selling expenses', so it should not be added separately.

This appears directly beneath 'Administrative expenses' and above 'Other Administrative expenses', indicating it is a component of the selected parent row 'Administrative expenses'. It also mixes D&A with impairment, so adding it separately would risk both double counting and scope contamination.
This appears to be a broader operating D&A total near Operating profit, not a clean SG&A-only line. Using it for XSGA_DA would likely overstate SG&A D&A and double count COGS-related D&A.
This is explicitly tied to COR/COGS, not SG&A.
